methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Electric Era, founded by Quincy Lee, is a Series A climate and energy company that has raised $11.5M to date. The valuation universe for frontier-energy companies includes one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers, among them Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

Stage-progression analysis, the only applicable triangulation method, yields a $23M implied valuation. This approach uses the $11.5M total capital raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement in the sector. The peer-median approach could not be applied; while nine same-sector comparables exist, none satisfied the filters for narrow peer set construction (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier), falling short of the three-datapoint threshold needed for reliable median extraction.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $20M to $26M, centered on $23M. This range reflects the inherent uncertainty of single-method triangulation and the thinness of narrowly-comparable data in frontier energy. Key uncertainties include Electric Era's specific technology maturity, capital efficiency relative to sector peers, and path to Series B readiness—factors not quantified in available data but material to stage-based valuation steps. Additional peers or disclosed metrics on burn rate and technology milestones would strengthen conviction in this range.
